name: Tag Intent
summary: A purely-decorative label/tag — a small inline chip that categorizes or annotates (a topic, a category, a keyword), distinct from the lifecycle Status Indicator and the count-bearing Notification Marker. Minted by #1319 (decompose-overloaded-vocabulary-by-semantic-source): same visual family, different semantic source. Decorative-only — it carries no lifecycle, no count, and no interaction; interactivity composes Action / Selection rather than being baked in.

tone.description: The semantic colour family — a category/severity hue, not an ad-hoc hex (the theme resolves it). Beyond the severity set, an open categorical/brand tone covers topic/keyword tags that map to no severity.

emphasis.description: Visual weight of the tag's fill — how strongly it reads against the surface.

shape.description: The visual metaphor for the tag token (reuses the Status Indicator vocabulary).

description:
What Tag Is

A decorative label — a small inline chip that categorizes or annotates (a topic, a category, a keyword, a count-less marker). It is the presentational sibling of the Status Indicator and the Notification Marker: same visual family, different semantic source (#1319, decompose-overloaded-vocabulary-by-semantic-source). Status Indicator reads a lifecycle; Notification Marker carries a count; Tag carries neither — it is meaning by category alone.

Decorative-only (Fork 2 = A)

Tag bakes in no interactivity. A removable filter-chip or a clickable category is a composition — the host element also carries an Action (a remove/activate control) or Selection (a toggle in a set); Tag stays the inert label underneath. This keeps the decorative axis from re-absorbing behavior it doesn't own.

Tone · Emphasis · Shape

Tone (default neutral) is a semantic colour family, not a hex; beyond the severity set, categorical covers topic/keyword tags that map to no severity. Emphasis (default subtle) is the fill weight — subtle / solid / outline. Shape reuses the Status Indicator vocabulary — badge / pill / tag. All three are optional with most-permissive defaults.

Accessibility

A tag is inert text. When it conveys meaning beyond colour (a category the sighted reader gets from the hue), it earns an accessible name so the meaning isn't colour-only. It is not announced via Live Region Status — a decorative label is not a live status change. Reproduction note: shadcn's badge remaps onto this intent (it was previously mis-mapped to Status Indicator). Feeds gap-sweep #315.
